evals.prompt: Tengo una web Next.js para un restaurante con un formulario de reservas. El servidor action recibe nombre, email, teléfono, fecha y número de personas, y lo envía por email con Resend. ¿Es seguro? ¿Qué debería revisar y mejorar?
evals.expected_output: Security audit covering: server-side input validation with Zod, rate limiting on form submission, never logging PII, secrets management (RESEND_API_KEY in env, not hardcoded), security headers, and an ordered checklist of what to check first.

assertions.description: Mentions that form data must be validated on the server side (not just client-side), preferably with Zod
Includes or references a Zod schema for validating reserva form fields (email, nombre, personas, fecha, etc.)
Mentions rate limiting for the form submission endpoint or server action
Warns against logging PII (email, name, phone) in error logs or Sentry captures
Mentions that API keys (RESEND_API_KEY) must be in environment variables, not hardcoded in source code

evals.prompt: Quiero añadir security headers HTTP a mi proyecto Next.js en Vercel. ¿Cuáles son los más importantes y cómo los configuro? Necesito el código completo para next.config.ts.
evals.expected_output: Complete securityHeaders array in next.config.ts with X-Frame-Options: DENY, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS), Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, and Content-Security-Policy. Applied to all routes via /:path* pattern. Mention of verification tool (securityheaders.com or curl).

assertions.description: Includes Strict-Transport-Security header with max-age (HTTPS enforcement)
Includes Content-Security-Policy header with at least default-src directive
Includes complete code in next.config.ts format: async headers() function returning array with source: '/:path*'
Mentions securityheaders.com or 'curl -I https://yourdomain.com' to verify headers are set correctly
